title: TeammateTool 13-operation protocol
description: spawnTeam, discoverTeams, requestJoin, approveJoin, rejectJoin, write, broadcast, requestShutdown, approveShutdown, rejectShutdown, approvePlan, rejectPlan, cleanup. Full lifecycle for persistent multi-agent teams with inbox messaging.

title: 6 orchestration patterns taxonomy
description: (1) Parallel Specialists — multiple reviewers simultaneously. (2) Pipeline — sequential with task dependencies. (3) Swarm — self-organizing from shared pool. (4) Research+Implementation — gather then apply. (5) Plan Approval — leader sign-off gate. (6) Coordinated Refactoring — multi-file with boundaries.

title: 3 spawn backends with auto-detection
description: in-process (same Node.js, hidden, fastest), tmux (separate processes, visible panes, persistent), iterm2 (macOS split panes). Auto-detects based on env vars and CLI availability. Configurable per team or globally.

title: Task dependency auto-unblock
description: Tasks have blockedBy arrays of task IDs. When a dependency completes, blocked tasks auto-unblock. Creates declarative pipelines without manual orchestration.

title: Text output NOT visible to team
description: Critical guideline: agent text output is NOT visible to teammates. MUST use write/broadcast to communicate. This is a common source of bugs in multi-agent workflows.

title: Subagent vs Teammate spawning distinction
description: Task tool alone = short-lived subagent, returns result directly. Task tool + team_name + name = persistent teammate with inbox, shared tasks, team lifecycle. Different tools for different coordination needs.

title: Graceful shutdown sequence
description: requestShutdown all agents -> wait for approveShutdown from each -> cleanup team resources. Handle crashed teammates by checking status before cleanup.

title: Plugin agent types from compound-engineering
description: Specialized reviewers beyond built-in types: security-sentinel, performance-oracle, architecture-strategist, code-simplicity-reviewer, and others. Available through plugin system.

title: Prefer write over broadcast
description: Best practice: targeted write to specific teammate is better than broadcast. Reduces noise, makes conversations trackable, prevents information overload in large teams.

title: No convergence or quality verification
description: Patterns describe how to dispatch and coordinate but not how to verify quality of agent outputs. No convergence loops, no verification passes, no output validation.

title: No compaction or state persistence
description: No treatment of what happens when agents hit context limits, how to checkpoint team state, or how to resume after failures.
